discussions.author: Zankye
discussions.text: the two biggest culprits for blades that drastically shifted the Beyblade X meta for Season 1 What is funny is how similar they are for length of impacting the meta and to what extent Phoenix Wing was super heavy so it would creep out most attack options, could be used on stamina combos and defense combos due to weight, it had a very strong presence in the first few months it had decent counters Unicorn Sting to KO or destabilize, Hells Scythe was kinda close, and eventually with UX Buster and Tyranno Beat it was hard countered for stamina spam. Wizard Rod is a heavy stamina type not as heavy as Phoenix Wing, it has a great shape and the current options were POSSIBLE to defeat it, a Buster 1-60 or attackers on 1-60 to KO it, but generally it is a big struggle to defeat. Things like using Rod on Hexa combos did not feel too bad because of the fact stamina could outspin it. But the Ball combo was very safe to play and annoying for a lot of players. With releases like Cobalt Dragoon and even Black Shell it feels like there are counters to it that do consistent. What is the common trend on both is that both of them have a STRONG impact on the meta but eventually something gets released that can defeat it, these did not feel overpowered in the sense the meta warps to them, it sometimes got close but always felt like it could be defeated eventually. I usually say if by 3-4 months they still are strong then maybe look at it for banning potentially or limiting parts.

discussions.author: azayakaph
discussions.text: I commissioned @bulletsshooting to design me a right spin CX Beyblade and here it is: The lock chip is Fox based on my logo since I like the fox motif. The main blade is Samurai similar to Dranbuster with Outward Weight Distribution(OWD) this creates an imbalanced weight setup that focuses power on one side allowing it to go for a one hit assassination playstyle - The assist blade is Katana - The ratchet is 1-60 - The bit is Rush with a plastic tip and metal gears to add extra weight and stability This combo is built to strike first and finish fast but the downside is it lacks stamina. #beybladex #azayakaph

discussions.author: DeltyThe73rd
discussions.text: someone asked me in DMs but I think it's fun to talk about it publicly since I love Beyblade X more often than not, I'd actually like to admit that the best attack type beyblades excel in their stock combo, yea I'm not kidding haha. I've played against Dranswords, Sharkedges and Drandaggers and they more or less pose a challenge competitively unless you're facing me and my Counterattack beys. ok but advice wise, I would suggest parts along the same line, with Dransword 360F (Flat), I would try using the later bit addition, Low Flat, that's used in stock Sharkedge. You can try mixing around too with the Rush but in Drandagger. Another thing I consider competitively is the ratchet where I think more or less, the lesser the protrusions the better AND the shortest one where the metal blades can attack other ratchets effectively so ideally I would use a 360 then 380 then 460 in a team setup.

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: Beyblade X Episode 08 - "The Mask and the King" (Part 1 - First half of Episode) The episode begins with a recap of the Persona vs. Zooganic match so far. Multi Nanairo won the first battle against Toguro Okunaga, but Bird Kazami suffered a crushing defeat against Cho Pan in the second battle. With the score tied 1-1, the deciding battle between Kamen X and King Manju is set to determine the winner. As the match begins, King confidently declares that he will tear off Kamen X’s mask along with his Bey, while Kamen X remains focused and unfazed. Leon Claw launches aggressively, striking fast and hard, putting Dran Sword on the defensive. However, X counters with Xtreme Dash, avoiding an early defeat. As the battle escalates, Kamen X matches King’s speed, leading to a high-speed collision between their Beys. The impact results in a simultaneous Over Finish—a draw! The audience is stunned, as this is the first time anyone has matched King’s power. With no winner yet, both Bladers prepare for a rematch.

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: Beyblade X Episode 28 - "The King and the Phoenix" (Filler) (Part 1 - First Half of Episode) Ichi-Go introduces the Shuffle Battle Fest, a 3-day event with randomly shuffled teams. It’s a big change of pace, focusing more on entertainment and audience voting than pure win-loss outcomes. This narrative detour blends tournament stakes with personality-driven pairings. Introduction of quirky team names like “King and Sushi,” “Ichigo-Pan,” and “Found Family.” Ichi-Go’s frantic but charming narration, offset by Zero-Go’s more grounded explanation of the Fest’s rules. A major revelation: votes decide the dream team and ultimate challengers, not just victories. Taisho and King’s banter reinforces their comedic chemistry. Burn’s quiet resolve and internal doubts are balanced by his fiery drive. Ichi-Go humorously joins as a participant, pushing the “AI mascot” trope into full character territory. The episode transitions seamlessly into the match between King Manju and Burn Fujiwara, with high production values in animation, particularly the Phoenix Wing vs Leon Claw clash. Their ideologies clash too—King’s imperial self-image vs Burn’s noble assertiveness.

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: Beyblade X Episode 02 - "Multi-Colored Assassin" This is the 2nd of 51 episodes of the first season. (Part 1 - First half of Episode) The episode opens with Bird reflecting on his goal to reach X Tower, but he doubts his current strength. X enthusiastically approaches him, coining the phrase "Bey day" to mean every day is a good day to battle. Bird questions why X left Team Pendragon, the strongest team, and X reveals that he wanted to battle Khrome Ryugu again—something he couldn’t do as a teammate. This showcases X’s carefree mindset: he sees Beyblade as fun, not work. Meanwhile, Kamen X starts gaining attention, but Bird is overshadowed, even being dismissed as a small fry by onlookers. Suddenly, Multi Nanairo, a famous influencer and talented Blader, arrives to compete solo in the Pro Selection match. Despite the disadvantage of being a one-person team, she has been winning consistently, making her a serious opponent. Bird realizes he doesn’t have a Bey for the match, but a mysterious woman offers him one from her massive collection: Hells Scythe. Though grateful, he later realizes she only gave it to him to make the battle more exciting, assuming he would lose. The match between Bird and Multi begins, and despite his efforts, Multi’s Wizard Arrow completely outmaneuvers him. Using Mirage Parry, she absorbs his strongest attack and redirects it, winning with an Over Finish.

discussions.author: Zankye
discussions.text: Pre Wizard Rod you had Hells Scythe, Wizard Arrow, Unicorn Sting, Phoenix Wing, arguably Phoenix Wing had a worse presence than Wizard Rod for that time but you still had 4 options There is more nuance to wanting Ghost to be heavy, have something that can compete and do well against Rod, these beys have different varying shapes, they will not perform the exact same, the same way we had 4 stamina beys that were all usable but each had their own pros and cons. You will always have one strong option or something that dominates over a period of time that is how Beyblade is, something strong one month may be bad for another month. What we can hope for as time keeps going is a diverse amount of options so we can play more than one type if we wanted to.

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: (Part 2 - Second Half of Episode) Kamen X dominates as usual, using his Xtreme Dash fake-outs to bait his opponent into a mistake. Multi showcases high-level tactics, tilting her Knight Shield Bey to utilize attacks to her advantage. The battles emphasize the importance of strategy, proving that reading the opponent is just as vital as raw power. After suffering through Kamen X’s horrible chocolate-dipped sushi, Bird finally gets his turn to make sushi. Surprise twist: Despite being a mediocre Blader, Bird turns out to be a sushi-making prodigy. His sushi technique is so refined and precise that even Komaba Sushi’s president is moved to tears. The revelation that Bird has more talent in sushi than Beyblade is both hilarious and tragic. The running joke of the episode is that everyone immediately assumes Bird should quit Beyblade and take over the sushi business. Multi and Kamen X jokingly plan his "retirement," much to his frustration. Despite his objections, the episode ends with Bird seemingly embracing his new skill, albeit reluctantly.

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: Beyblade X Episode 14 - "Ekusu Exercise" (Filler) Note: The only thing about this episode that is cannon is when Kadovar mentions that Burn Fujiwara is the one that beat his fastest time. You could say this episode adapted chapter 6 (pages 47-48), but the location of this being mentioned is different in the anime compared to the manga. (Part 1 - First half of Episode) The episode opens with a flashback of a mysterious conversation between two powerful Bladers at the top of X Tower. They discuss how they've reached the summit and now must defend their title rather than fight for it. One of them, feeling restless, questions if being at the top is truly fun. Back in the present, Ekusu wakes up groggy and hungry, staring at his spinning sushi plate. The team starts the day with Taisho’s… unique sushi creations, including Salmon Custard Sushi, Garlic Cherry Cake Sushi, and Habanero Sauce Mango Tart Sushi. Multi and Bird struggle but try to stomach it, while Ekusu happily devours everything. Meiko Meiden points out that Ekusu has been slacking lately, both physically and mentally. While he protests ("Do I look fat?!?"), Meiko informs him that someone has broken his fastest completion time for Kadovar’s Test. This news shocks the team. They don’t know who this new Blader is yet, but it’s a reminder that records are meant to be broken. Meiko implements a strict training regimen for Team Persona. Strength Training: Ekusu barely lifts any weight, clearly unmotivated. Sprinting & Endurance: Bird is excelling, but Ekusu struggles to keep up. Meiko explains that this training isn’t just for Ekusu—it’s also for Bird. While Bird always fights with brute force, he needs to understand different training methods and strategies from others. She also wants Ekusu to learn to battle under different conditions—ones that challenge his usual attack-based strategy. Meiko forces Ekusu to battle using Knight Shield, a Defense Type Bey, while she takes control of Dran Sword, his usual Bey. The team wonders how Ekusu will handle a Defense Type for the first time. Despite expectations that he’ll go for a standard defensive strategy, Ekusu surprises everyone by playing aggressively. In a shocking twist, he jumps his Bey even higher than Meiko’s attack, securing an Over Finish victory. Meiko praises him for proving that battle instincts are just as important as training.

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: Beyblade X Episode 04 - "Bey Sponsor" This is the 4th of 51 episodes of the first season. (Part 1 - First half of Episode) Team Persona is riding high after their recent promotion to Pro status, celebrating at a yakiniku restaurant. Multi, always dramatic, complains about the smell getting on her clothes, while Bird is in awe that they are finally standing on the same battlefield as top Pro Bladers. However, their celebration is cut short when Multi drops a bombshell—they're not officially Pros yet. The episode introduces a crucial part of the Beyblade X world: Bey Sponsors. Multi explains that Pro Bladers must have a sponsor, as they provide training grounds and financial support in exchange for advertising their brand. The system mirrors real-world sponsorships in sports, adding an interesting layer of realism. Multi, always ahead of the game, reveals that she has already secured a potential sponsor for them. Excited, Bird and Kamen X assume it must be a big corporation, but their expectations are shattered when they arrive at a humble sushi restaurant: Komaba Sushi. Here, they meet Taisho Sushiya, the eccentric owner and president of Komaba Sushi, and his assistant Meiko Meiden. Taisho, a passionate sushi chef, reveals that he once aspired to be a Pro Blader but had to take over his family’s business instead. Now, through sponsoring Team Persona, he hopes to live out his dream vicariously. However, there’s a catch—they must first pass a Pro Test to prove they are worthy of his sponsorship. The test takes place in Komaba Sushi’s basement stadium, which Taisho proudly renovated (though he admits it put them back into financial trouble). The challenge? Bird and Multi must defeat Meiko Meiden in a Beybattle, with at least one of them securing a win. Kamen X, ever the wildcard, insists on battling too, so the requirement changes—now they must win two out of three matches to pass the test. Ekusu steps up first, facing off against Meiko’s Shark Edge. However, the battle ends in an instant—Dran Sword knocks Shark Edge out of the stadium with a brutal Over Finish. The speed and power difference is staggering, but Meiko doesn’t seem upset. Instead, her reaction suggests she anticipated this result. Next, it’s Bird vs. Meiko. Bird fights hard, using Hells Scythe’s quick attacks to pressure Shark Edge, but his overaggressive approach leaves him wide open. Meiko counters effortlessly, sending his Bey flying with a Burst Finish. The true twist comes after the match—Meiko reveals that she is a former Pro Blader! She had already calculated a 96.9% chance of losing to Ekusu and a 0% chance of losing to Bird. Her strategy was clear—she threw her match against Ekusu on purpose to conserve her energy for the real test. With Bird eliminated, the pressure falls on Multi to secure their sponsorship. But Meiko drops another revelation: she has analyzed Multi’s previous matches and concluded that Knight Shield has a 0% chance of winning. Unfazed, Multi reveals her new Beyblade, Knight Lance. This moment reinforces one of her key strengths: adaptability. Multi thrives in high-pressure situations by constantly changing her approach, and this time is no different. As the final match begins, Meiko once again tries to overpower her opponent with Blue Fang, but Multi’s strategy counters it perfectly. She tilts her launch to give Knight Lance a swaying motion, allowing it to dodge Shark Edge’s devastating strikes. Then, using Rainy Lance, she strikes from above, flipping the momentum of the match. The battle ends with a decisive Over Finish, and Multi wins, securing Team Persona’s sponsorship!

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: (Part 2 - Second half of Episode) Just when it seems like Kamen X is out of options, he unveils his true strategy: Double Xtreme Dash. This move is not just about speed—it’s a calculated trap. Throughout the battle, Multi assumes her Knight Shield’s defensive stance has completely neutralized Kamen X’s offensive capabilities. She even taunts him, believing she has successfully outlasted his attack power. However, Kamen X has been baiting her all along, using the first Xtreme Dash to make her drop her guard. The execution of this moment is fantastic. The slow-motion effect, followed by the shock on Multi’s face, highlights how instantly the tide turns. Kamen X uses his Bey’s insane speed to ricochet off the rail, strike Knight Shield from an unpredictable angle, and send it flying out of the stadium. It’s an Over Finish, securing Team Persona’s victory and officially making Bird and Kamen X Pro Bladers. Multi’s Reaction: From Defeat to Team Persona’s Newest Member Multi, despite her confidence, is visibly shaken by the loss. In an over-the-top display of emotion, she breaks down into tears, wailing dramatically as the crowd watches in disbelief. This moment is hilarious because of how quickly her personality shifts—one second she’s a confident showman, and the next, she’s crying like a child. The anime plays into this well, exaggerating her expressions and making it a comedic highlight. But just as fast as she crumbles into despair, Multi snaps back to her usual self when Kamen X and Bird invite her to join Team Persona. Her mood whiplash is one of the most entertaining parts of the episode—she instantly transitions from sobbing to proudly declaring herself their new teammate, as if she was never upset to begin with. This moment reinforces Multi’s unpredictable and theatrical personality. She’s not just about winning battles; she’s about creating a spectacle. Her charisma and energy make her a perfect fit for Team Persona, adding a wild card dynamic to the team. Meanwhile, at the Bey Café, Multi decides to live-streaming their victory, further emphasizing the modern, social media-driven world of Beyblade X. Her influence as a popular Blader and streamer is fully on display, as thousands of viewers flood her stream with reactions and Bey Stars (virtual rewards). More importantly, Multi immediately begins crafting Kamen X’s public image. Instead of making him just another well-known Blader, she turns his mysterious persona into a marketing strategy. By limiting his exposure and keeping his battle style a secret, she builds intrigue around him. This ensures that whenever Kamen X steps into the stadium, audiences will be dying to see what he does next. This is a brilliant move, showcasing how in Beyblade X, success isn’t just about being a strong Blader—it’s also about how well you market yourself. Multi understands that hype and reputation matter just as much as actual skill. Her branding tactics ensure that Team Persona will be the most talked-about team going forward. Meanwhile, Bird experiences his first true taste of Pro-level competition. Despite his official promotion, he can’t ignore the massive skill gap between himself and top Bladers. While Kamen X and Multi exude confidence, Bird is left grappling with self-doubt. His internal conflict is subtle but significant—he knows he has the potential to be great, but right now, he’s still struggling to keep up. This sets up a compelling arc for him going forward. While Multi and Kamen X thrive in the spotlight, Bird has to fight for his place in the team, proving that he deserves to stand among them.

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: Beyblade X Episode 03 - "Team Persona" This is the 3rd of 51 episodes of the first season. (Part 1 - First half of Episode) Kamen X enters the match as Team Persona’s last hope, and Multi, ever the showwoman, decides to change her Bey from Wizard Arrow to Knight Shield. This moment highlights one of the key advantages Multi has over most Bladers—her ability to switch Beys mid-battle to fit the situation. Her transformation and costume change also add to her dramatic flair, making her one of the most entertaining characters so far. The anime does a great job of explaining the Beyblade type matchups—Attack beats Stamina, Stamina beats Defense, and Defense beats Attack. Kamen X quickly realizes that he’s at a disadvantage, but instead of backing down, he declares that he’ll end the battle in the blink of an eye. This moment perfectly captures his cocky but confident personality, setting up the battle with high stakes. Once the match begins, Multi’s defensive tactics shine. Despite Kamen X’s relentless attacks, Knight Shield doesn’t budge, showcasing the power of a well-used Defense Type Bey. Even Xtreme Dash fails to break through. However, Kamen X isn’t just blindly attacking—he’s aiming for something bigger.

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: Beyblade X Episode 07 - "Team Zooganic" This is the 7th of 51 episodes of the first season. (Part 1 - First half of Episode) The episode kicks off with Team Persona's official Pro debut match against Team Zooganic, one of the most hyped rookie teams in the X Tower. The buildup is strong, reinforcing Bird’s nervousness as he steps onto the grand stage. Multi and Jaxon remain composed, emphasizing their experience in comparison. Multi is the first up to battle against Toguro Okunaga, and his mind games start immediately. He taunts Multi by asking her to use an Attack Type, seemingly setting himself up for failure. But as a strategist, he has something up his sleeve—his Bey, Viper Tail, is a Stamina Type that gradually chips away at its opponent’s endurance through a relentless barrage of minor hits. Multi, however, decides to take a risk and chooses to battle with Wizard Arrow, a Stamina Type, rather than countering with an Attack Type. This decision shocks even her teammates, making it clear that she has a larger goal beyond just winning—she wants to put on a show. The animation in this segment is solid, with Viper Tail’s “coiling” movements adding a unique visual identity to Toguro’s strategy. The tension rises as Viper Tail's tactic begins taking effect, slowly whittling down Wizard Arrow’s spin. Toguro appears confident, believing he’s in control. Multi realizes she may have walked straight into Toguro’s trap, as Stamina Types are Viper Tail’s favorite prey.

discussions.author: DranzerX13Anime
discussions.text: Beyblade X Episode 09 - "Beycrafter" (Part 1 - First half of Episode) The episode opens with Bird Kazami’s loss against Cho Pan being analyzed. While his Bey, Hells Scythe, is strong enough to win matches, Bird has yet to master it. Multi and Ekusu discuss training Bird to improve his skill, but Bird sarcastically asks how many years that will take. Multi suggests that before training, they should analyze Bird’s battle style. To do this, she asks for his Bey Battle Pass. However, Bird is completely unaware of what that is, confusing both Multi and Ekusu. The Bey Battle Pass is introduced as a tracking device attached to a launcher, which records data such as launch power, spin rate, and match statistics. Bird, having no idea what it does, let his battery die, rendering all his past data useless. This frustrates Multi, as they now have to start analyzing his style from scratch. As they discuss training, Team Persona receives an invitation to the Bey Café’s 5th-anniversary event. The café, known for its high status in X City, wants Persona to participate in an exhibition battle. Multi and Ekusu are specifically requested for the event, leaving Bird disappointed that he wasn’t invited. Multi encourages Bird to join the battle anyway, explaining that as a Pro, he needs to put on an entertaining match, not just focus on winning. Bird doesn’t fully understand, but he agrees. Multi unveils a Bey Machine, a device that creates custom Beyblades using precise data analysis. Bird is shocked to learn that only a licensed Beycrafter can operate it, and Multi has such a license. The team decides that instead of forcing Bird to adjust to Hells Scythe, they will craft a new Bey that matches his natural battle style. Bird hesitates at first, but after Multi reassures him that this is only to help him grow, he agrees to the idea. Multi spends countless hours working on crafting Bird’s new Bey. Bird, noticing how hard she’s working, secretly prepares food for her, making easy-to-eat snacks. Multi is touched by the gesture, and the two share a heartfelt moment. After pulling an all-nighter, Multi finally completes Bird's new Bey, Hells Chain!
